prompt.body:
## Goal
Installing the factory plugin delivers the operating manual and nothing else — no tool-server registration rides along to every consumer machine.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL ship no `.mcp.json` at the repository root.
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL register no MCP servers in the plugin packaging (`.claude-plugin/`).
- [ ] THE SYSTEM SHALL guard both facts with a packaging test that fails on reintroduction.

## Context & links
Delete `.mcp.json` from the factory repo root. The factory skill is distributed as a git-sourced plugin — the plugin is the whole repo — so the repo-root `.mcp.json` (a playwright MCP server config, `npx @playwright/mcp@latest`) silently registers a tool server with **every consumer of the plugin on every machine** (census B.3 finding 3). It serves no factory function: no factory tool uses playwright, and pipeline stages must not load MCP servers at all (the stage-isolation slice makes stages immune via `--strict-mcp-config` regardless — this task fixes *consumers*). Remove the file and add a small packaging guard test under `tests/` (pytest, alongside the suite run by `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q`) asserting the repo **ships** no `.mcp.json` and that `.claude-plugin/` (the plugin manifest directory, `plugin.json`) registers no MCP servers — the guard is what makes the acceptance criterion durable against reintroduction. Guard against the **tracked tree** (`git ls-files` or equivalent shipped-content check), not the working filesystem: `.gitignore:7` already lists `.mcp.json`, so after the deletion a legitimate untracked local copy is both invited and invisible — a filesystem-existence assertion would fail a dev machine while CI stays green. Verify with a repo grep that nothing else references the file; no other behavior changes.

## Test expectations
The guard test itself: the tracked tree ships no `.mcp.json` at the repo root (assert via `git ls-files`, not filesystem existence — an untracked gitignored local copy must not fail the suite); the plugin manifest declares no MCP servers. Existing suite untouched and green.

## Constraints / out of scope
Nothing else changes. Stage-side MCP immunity is the stage-isolation slice's, not this task's. Do not touch the operator host's own plugin configuration (attended companion work).

## Size
S — one PR

held_out_tests.content:
"""Packaging guard for issue #51 — the plugin ships the manual, registers nothing.

The factory skill is distributed as a git-sourced plugin (the plugin is the whole
repo), so anything the *tracked tree* ships rides along to every consumer machine.
These tests guard the tracked tree (`git ls-files`), not the working filesystem:
`.gitignore` invites a legitimate untracked local `.mcp.json`, so a filesystem
existence check would fail on a dev machine while CI stayed green.
"""

import json
import pathlib
import subprocess

ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


def _tracked_files():
    out = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "-C", str(ROOT), "ls-files"],
        check=True, capture_output=True, text=True,
    )
    return out.stdout.splitlines()


def test_repo_ships_no_mcp_json_at_root():
    tracked = _tracked_files()
    assert ".mcp.json" not in tracked


def test_no_tracked_mcp_json_anywhere():
    tracked = _tracked_files()
    mcp_files = [p for p in tracked if pathlib.PurePosixPath(p).name == ".mcp.json"]
    assert mcp_files == []


def test_plugin_manifest_registers_no_mcp_servers():
    tracked = _tracked_files()
    plugin_dir_files = [p for p in tracked if p.startswith(".claude-plugin/")]
    assert plugin_dir_files, "expected a tracked .claude-plugin/ manifest directory"

    for relpath in plugin_dir_files:
        path = ROOT / relpath
        if path.suffix != ".json":
            continue
        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        assert "mcpServers" not in data, f"{relpath} registers an MCP server"
        assert "mcp_servers" not in data, f"{relpath} registers an MCP server"


def test_plugin_manifest_has_no_mcp_config_file():
    tracked = _tracked_files()
    plugin_dir_files = [p for p in tracked if p.startswith(".claude-plugin/")]
    mcp_config_files = [p for p in plugin_dir_files if "mcp" in pathlib.PurePosixPath(p).name.lower()]
    assert mcp_config_files == []
